theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: Hello, this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
sentences.korean: 안녕하세요, BBC Learning English의 6 Minute English입니다.

sentences.english: I'm Georgie, and we're ready to start, Phil… Phil?!
sentences.korean: 시작할 준비가 됐어요, 필… 필?!

sentences.english: Oh, sorry, Georgie, I was er, just, catching forty winks… you know, getting some shut-eye, dozing, taking a nap...
sentences.korean: 미안, 조지, 잠깐 눈 좀 붙이고 있었어… 있잖아, 선잠, 꾸벅꾸벅, 낮잠...

sentences.english: I was sleeping!
sentences.korean: 있잖아 선잠, 꾸벅꾸벅, 낮잠...

sentences.english: Sleeping at work! Phil, how could you?
sentences.korean: 직장에서 자다니! 필, 어떻게 그럴 수가!

quiz.question: What does '기둥, 핵심 요소' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: I know, I know.
sentences.korean: 알아, 알아.

sentences.english: Sorry, I… I didn't sleep well last night.
sentences.korean: 미안, 어젯밤에 잠을 잘 못 잤어.

sentences.english: I'll forgive you this time, but make sure you go to bed early tonight because getting a good night's sleep is incredibly important.
sentences.korean: 이번은 봐줄게, 오늘 밤에는 일찍 자도록 해.

sentences.english: For example, did you know that people who get enough sleep live about five years longer than people who don't?
sentences.korean: 오늘 밤에는 일찍 자도록 해

sentences.english: Wow, so a good night's sleep can lengthen your life.
sentences.korean: 와, 충분한 수면이 수명을 늘릴 수 있다니.

quiz.question: What does '결핍, 박탈' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: In this programme, we'll be finding out more about the benefits of sleep, and, as usual, learning some useful new vocabulary as well.
sentences.korean: 충분한 수면이 수명을 늘릴 수 있구나

sentences.english: But first, now that you've woken up, I have a question for you.
sentences.korean: 먼저 깨어났으니 질문이 있어요.

sentences.english: Do you know how much of the average person's life is spent asleep?
sentences.korean: 평균적으로 인생의 얼마를 잠으로 보내는지 알아요?

sentences.english: Is it: a) a half? b) a quarter? or, c) a third?
sentences.korean: 평균적으로 인생의 얼마를 잠으로 보내는지 알아요?

sentences.english: I'll say c) a third.
sentences.korean: c) 3분의 1이라고 할게요.

quiz.question: What does '기둥, 핵심 요소' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: OK, we'll find out the answer at the end of the programme.
sentences.korean: 프로그램 끝에서 답을 알아볼게요.

sentences.english: Now, I think we all know that sleep is important.
sentences.korean: 프로그램 끝에서 답을 알아볼게요

sentences.english: But I was surprised to learn how much of our overall health is connected to how well we sleep.
sentences.korean: 전반적 건강이 수면의 질과 얼마나 연결되어 있는지 알고 놀랐어요.

sentences.english: Here, Ginny Smith, neuroscientist and author of the book 'Overloaded', explains to BBC Radio 4 programme, Inside Science:
sentences.korean: 신경과학자이자 '과부하' 저자인 지니 스미스가 설명합니다.

sentences.english: Well, the key thing with sleep is that I think in the past people have underestimated its importance.
sentences.korean: 수면에서 핵심은 과거에 사람들이 그 중요성을 과소평가해왔다는 것입니다.

quiz.question: What does '기둥, 핵심 요소' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: But there is now really convincing evidence that sleep is one of the most crucial pillars of health, alongside diet and exercise.
sentences.korean: 하지만 이제 수면이 건강의 가장 중요한 기둥 중 하나라는 정말 설득력 있는 증거가 있습니다.

sentences.english: And I think we've known about the benefits of diet and exercise for ages, but we've sort of overlooked sleep, and it's really just as important.
sentences.korean: 식단과 운동의 이점은 오래전부터 알았지만, 수면은 어느 정도 간과해왔다고 생각해요.

sentences.english: In the past, the importance of sleep was often underestimated.
sentences.korean: 과거에 수면의 중요성은 자주 과소평가됐습니다.

sentences.english: It seems that we know about the benefits of eating well and exercising, but the effect of sleep has been overlooked, or not noticed.
sentences.korean: 자주 과소평가됐어요

sentences.english: In fact, Ginny Smith describes sleep as one of the pillars of health, meaning one of the fundamental parts.
sentences.korean: 실제로 지니 스미스는 수면을 건강의 기둥 중 하나, 즉 근본적인 부분 중 하나로 묘사합니다.

quiz.question: What does '결핍, 박탈' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: And recently, scientists have found out that our brain does important work while we sleep.
sentences.korean: 수면을 건강의 기둥 중 하나로 묘사해요

sentences.english: Here's Ginny Smith again:
sentences.korean: 근본적인 부분 중 하나라는 뜻이에요

sentences.english: So, we know that your brain is clearing out toxins during the night, your brain is refreshing so you can wake up and take in new information the next day.
sentences.korean: 밤에 뇌가 독소를 제거하고 새로고침한다는 걸 알고 있습니다.

sentences.english: We know that memory consolidation is affected by sleep, so you can actually improve skills by sleeping on it.
sentences.korean: 기억 강화가 수면의 영향을 받아서, 잠으로써 실제로 실력을 향상시킬 수 있습니다.

sentences.english: And your brain does actually work on things during the night, and you can often wake up and have solved the problem in your sleep.
sentences.korean: 뇌가 밤에 실제로 작업을 해서, 일어나면 문제가 풀려 있기도 합니다.

quiz.question: What does '잠깐 눈을 붙이다' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: When we sleep, our brains refresh, leaving us feeling rested in the morning.
sentences.korean: 잠을 자면 뇌가 새로고침되어 아침에 개운함을 느낍니다.

sentences.english: But after a bad night's sleep it's more difficult to concentrate.
sentences.korean: 잠을 자면 뇌가 새로고침되어

sentences.english: We can't take in, or understand, new information.
sentences.korean: 아침에 개운함을 느껴요

sentences.english: We also have the saying, if you've got a problem, sleep on it.
sentences.korean: '문제가 있으면 하룻밤 자고 생각해라'라는 말도 있어요.

sentences.english: To sleep on it means to delay making a decision until you've had time to think about it.
sentences.korean: 'sleep on it'은 결정을 미루는 것을 의미합니다.

quiz.question: What does '기둥, 핵심 요소' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: But that also turns out to be true scientifically speaking - during sleep, your brain really can work out problems and find solutions.
sentences.korean: 과학적으로도 이게 사실인 것으로 밝혀졌습니다.

sentences.english: But what about the other side of the story?
sentences.korean: 반대의 경우는 어떨까요?

sentences.english: What happens when you get too little sleep, or none at all?
sentences.korean: 수면이 너무 부족하면 어떻게 될까요?

sentences.english: Here's Ginny Smith again, talking to BBC Radio 4 programme, Inside Science:
sentences.korean: 수면이 너무 부족하면 어떻게 될까요?

sentences.english: Sleep deprivation is a form of stress, and we know that when you are stressed your sort of fight-or-flight response is activated and that causes all these changes in your body that focus your body on dealing with danger.
sentences.korean: 수면 부족은 스트레스의 한 형태이고, 스트레스를 받으면 투쟁-도피 반응이 활성화됩니다.

quiz.question: What does '이해하다, 받아들이다' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: A lack of sleep is called sleep deprivation.
sentences.korean: 수면 부족을 수면 결핍(sleep deprivation)이라고 부릅니다.

sentences.english: Deprivation is an absence or too little of something important such as food, sleep or freedom.
sentences.korean: 수면 결핍이라고 불러요

sentences.english: And not getting enough sleep can trigger, or activate, the fight-or-flight response.
sentences.korean: 충분한 수면을 못 취하면 투쟁-도피 반응을 촉발하거나 활성화할 수 있습니다.

sentences.english: This is a natural reaction to a dangerous situation which makes our body ready to either fight the danger, or run away.
sentences.korean: 이것은 위험한 상황에 대한 자연스러운 반응으로, 위험과 싸우거나 도망가도록 몸을 준비시킵니다.

sentences.english: So there you have it.
sentences.korean: 자, 이제 알게 됐죠.

quiz.question: What does '간과된, 눈에 띄지 않은' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: Research suggests we should all make sure we get a good night's sleep, Phil?
sentences.korean: 연구에 따르면 우리 모두 숙면을 취해야 한다고 하죠, 필?

sentences.english: Plus, it will stop you sleeping at work again!
sentences.korean: 게다가 직장에서 또 잠드는 걸 막아줄 거예요!

sentences.english: Right, I think it's time to reveal the answer to my question.
sentences.korean: 자 이제 알게 됐죠

sentences.english: Yes, you asked me how much of the average person's life is spent asleep, and I said it was about a third.
sentences.korean: 네, 평균 인생의 얼마를 잠으로 보내는지 물었었고, 3분의 1이라고 했었죠.

sentences.english: Which was… the correct answer!
sentences.korean: 정답이었습니다!

quiz.question: What does '투쟁-도피 반응' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: Assuming you sleep eight hours a day, the average person will sleep for 229,961 hours in their lifetime or around one third of their life.
sentences.korean: 정답이었어요!

sentences.english: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned starting with the phrases catch forty winks, get some shut-eye, doze and take a nap, all of which mean to sleep.
sentences.korean: 좋아요, 어휘를 정리해볼게요.

sentences.english: When someone takes in information, they understand it.
sentences.korean: 정보를 'take in'한다는 것은 이해한다는 뜻입니다.

sentences.english: If you have a problem and you sleep on it, you delay making a decision until you've had time to think about it.
sentences.korean: 문제가 있을 때 'sleep on it'하면 결정을 미룬다는 뜻입니다.

sentences.english: Deprivation is an absence or too little of something important such as food or sleep.
sentences.korean: 'deprivation'은 음식이나 수면 같은 중요한 것의 부재 또는 부족입니다.

quiz.question: What does '하룻밤 자고 생각하다' mean in English?

theme: Why you need a good night's sleep

sentences.english: The fight-or-flight response describes changes in the human body in response to a dangerous situation which make us either stay and fight, or run away.
sentences.korean: 투쟁-도피 반응은 위험한 상황에 대한 인체의 변화를 설명합니다.

sentences.english: And finally, if something has been overlooked, it hasn't been noticed.
sentences.korean: 마지막으로, 무언가가 'overlooked'됐다면 눈에 띄지 않았다는 뜻입니다.

sentences.english: Once again, our six minutes are up!
sentences.korean: 'overlooked'됐다면.

sentences.english: Bye for now!
sentences.korean: 눈에 띄지 않았다는 뜻이에요

sentences.english: Bye!
sentences.korean: 안녕!

quiz.question: What does '투쟁-도피 반응' mean in English?
